origin.question: [Êñá‰ª∂ 1: 1_s15_15.8.md]Problem 15.8. A rocket ship in a circular orbit of circumference $2\pi r$ around a star of mass M fires a laser gun (rest frequency $\nu_0$ ). The gun is aimed in the orbital plane, and at an angle $\alpha$ (in the ship's frame) outward from the tangential direction of motion. What is the frequency of the laser as seen by a stationary observer at infinity?## Solution
origin.answer: \nu_ {\mathrm {S O I}} = \gamma \nu_ {0} (1 + \mathrm {v} \cos \alpha) (1 - 2 \mathrm {M} / \mathrm {r}) ^ {\frac {1}{2}}where $\mathbf{v}$ is given by Equation (4).
